children.text: One sunny afternoon, I visited the park with my younger brother to play games.
children.cn: 一个晴朗的下午，我和弟弟去公园玩游戏。
children.notes: afternoon: 下午；visited: 参观/拜访（visit的过去式）；younger brother: 弟弟
children.statement: visited 是规则动词过去式。

children.text: While we were playing with a ball, it suddenly rolled into a deep, narrow hole.
children.cn: 正当我们在玩球时，它突然滚进了一个又深又窄的洞里。
children.statement: narrow 是形容词，意为“狭窄的”。

children.text: My brother felt very upset because he could not reach it.
children.cn: 我弟弟感到很沮丧，因为他够不着球。
children.notes: upset: 沮丧的，难过的；reach: 够到
children.statement: could 是 can 的过去式。

children.text: After thinking carefully for a moment, I came up with a clever idea.
children.cn: 仔细思考了一会儿后，我想到了一个聪明的点子。
children.notes: thinking carefully: 仔细思考；moment: 片刻；came up with: 想出
children.statement: carefully 是副词，用来修饰 thinking。

children.text: I quickly ran to a water fountain and brought a bucket of water.
children.cn: 我飞快地跑到饮水机旁，提来了一桶水。
children.notes: fountain: 喷泉（这里指饮水机）；brought: 带来（bring的过去式）；bucket: 水桶

children.text: I poured it into the hole little by little.
children.cn: 我一点一点地把水倒进洞里。
children.notes: little by little: 一点一点地
children.statement: little by little 意为“逐渐地/一点点地”。
children.explanation: 常用于描述渐进的过程。

children.text: To our surprise, the water made the ball float up.
children.cn: 令我们惊讶的是，水让球浮了上来。
children.notes: to our surprise: 令我们惊讶的是

children.text: My brother's eyes were bright and he became happy again.
children.cn: 我弟弟眼亮了起来，他又变得开心了。
children.notes: bright: 明亮的；became: 变得（become的过去式）
children.statement: became 是 become 的过去式。

children.text: I felt really proud of myself for working it out.
children.cn: 我为自己解决了问题感到非常自豪。
children.notes: proud of: 为...自豪；working it out: 解决问题
children.statement: working it out 使用了动名词形式作介词 for 的宾语。

children.text: I realized that thinking carefully is the best way to solve problems.
children.cn: 我意识到，仔细思考是解决问题的最好方式。
